home.heroSubtitle: A premier Islamic institution in Uttar Pradesh, India — dedicated to Quranic education, Islamic sciences, and nurturing the next generation of scholars since 1999.
home.aboutDesc: Established in Husianabad, Mau, Uttar Pradesh, India in 1999, Madrasa Farooqia has been a beacon of authentic Islamic knowledge for over 25 years. We nurture students from across India and the world in Quran, Hadith, Fiqh, and Arabic sciences.
home.verse1_trans: Read in the name of your Lord who created.
home.verse2_trans: Whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out.
home.verse3_trans: Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
home.branch1_desc: Full Islamic curriculum — Alim course, Arabic, Tajweed, and primary education for boys. Est. 1999.
home.branch2_desc: Dedicated Islamic education for girls — Hifz, Alimah course, Tajweed in a fully segregated environment. Est. 2003.
home.branch3_desc: The spiritual heart — daily prayers, dedicated Hifz program with Ijazah-chain teachers. Est. 1999.
home.branchesDesc: Madrasa Farooqia operates three distinct institutions — each serving a unique need of the Muslim community.
home.ctaDescription: Every donation, big or small, helps us provide quality Islamic education to students in need.

niswaan.title: Niswaan Branch
niswaan.hero_verse_trans: "And He taught you what you did not know." — Quran 4:113
niswaan.hero_desc: A dedicated, fully-registered Islamic education institution for Muslim girls — offering a safe, nurturing environment rooted in Quran, Sunnah, and scholarship since 2003.
niswaan.founding_title: Founded in 2003 — By Community, For Community
niswaan.founding_p1: In 2003, Hazrat Maulana Mohammad Farooq Sahib, founder of Madrasa Farooqia, recognised a critical gap — the Muslim girls of Mau and surrounding districts had no access to quality Islamic education in a safe, fully segregated environment.
niswaan.founding_p2: He established Niswaan Branch with a founding batch of 25 students, a dedicated female principal, and a clear mission: to produce Alimaat, Hafizaat, and educated Muslim women who would serve as pillars of their families, communities, and the ummah.
niswaan.founding_p3: Today, Niswaan serves over 300 students from UP, Bihar, MP, and beyond — many on full scholarship. Every aspect of governance, finance, and academic standards is maintained with full transparency.
niswaan.founding_accent: Niswaan holds UBSM affiliation, 80G tax exemption, and is registered under UP Waqf Board — making all donations tax-deductible.
niswaan.docs_desc: All our registrations and legal documents are public. We believe full transparency builds trust.
niswaan.verify_docs_desc: All original documents are available for inspection at our office. You can also contact the respective authorities to verify registration numbers. Email: info@madrasafarooqia.org
niswaan.milestones_desc: 21 years of growth, transparency, and service to the community
niswaan.mile1: Niswaan Branch officially established within Madrasa Farooqia premises
niswaan.mile2: First batch of 25 students enrolled in Hifz and Alimah programs
niswaan.mile3: Dedicated 3-storey building constructed for girls' section — fully segregated
niswaan.mile4: First Hifz graduation — 8 Hafizaat completed 30 Juz with Ijazah
niswaan.mile5: Alimah program expanded to full 7-year Dars-e-Nizami curriculum
niswaan.mile6: Computer literacy and Islamic calligraphy classes added
niswaan.mile7: FCRA registration obtained — enabling international donations
niswaan.mile8: Student count crossed 300 — new residential wing opened
niswaan.mile9: Online classes launched during COVID-19 — serving 200+ students digitally
niswaan.mile10: New science lab and library wing dedicated for Niswaan students
niswaan.mile11: Scholarship fund launched — 50 underprivileged girls receive full fee waiver
niswaan.faculty_desc: All female teaching staff — qualified Islamic scholars with Ijazah
niswaan.courses_desc: Structured Islamic academic programs for Muslim girls of all ages
niswaan.course1_desc: Complete memorisation of the Holy Quran with Tajweed rules
niswaan.course2_desc: Full Islamic scholarship curriculum for girls — Hadith, Fiqh, Tafsir, Arabic
niswaan.course3_desc: Correct pronunciation, makhaarij, and rules of recitation
niswaan.course4_desc: Aqeedah, Fiqh, Seerah, Islamic Ethics & Arabic language
niswaan.course5_desc: Quran Qaida, basic prayers, Islamic etiquette for young girls
niswaan.faq1_a: Niswaan Branch was founded in 2003 by Hazrat Maulana Mohammad Farooq Sahib, the founder of Madrasa Farooqia, in response to community demand for quality Islamic education for girls in a safe, segregated environment.
niswaan.faq2_a: Yes. Niswaan has a completely separate entrance, building, and facilities. Male family members are only permitted in the designated visitors area. All staff are qualified female scholars.
niswaan.faq3_a: Yes. We provide full and partial scholarships for families below the poverty line. Contact us for the scholarship application form. We never turn away a student due to financial hardship.
niswaan.faq4_q: What government approvals does Niswaan have?
niswaan.faq4_a: Niswaan is registered with the UP Waqf Board, UP Madrasa Education Board (UBSM), Registrar of Societies, and holds 80G tax exemption and FCRA registration.
niswaan.support_girls_education_desc: Your donation funds scholarships, teacher salaries, books, and infrastructure for the Niswaan Branch.

masjid.title: Masjid & Hifz Program
masjid.hero_verse_trans: "Indeed, it is We who sent down the Quran and indeed, We will be its guardian." — Quran 15:9
masjid.hero_desc: A dedicated, fully-registered Quran memorisation institution operating inside Masjid Farooqia — offering Ijazah-chain instruction and structured revision paths since 1999.
masjid.founding_p1: In 1999, Hazrat Maulana Mohammad Farooq Sahib established Masjid Farooqia as the spiritual heart of the campus. He knew that Quranic memorisation is not merely academic, but a spiritual covenant requiring discipline, prayer, and direct teacher-to-student connection.
masjid.founding_p2: The Hifz program was launched with 15 students in the Masjid. Over the past 25 years, under Hazrat's supervision and rigorous testing standards, the program has produced over 500 qualified Huffaz with strong retention.
masjid.founding_p3: All students undergo daily examination of new lessons (Sabaq) and revision of past lessons (Manzil). Certified teachers holding Ijazah chains back to the Prophet (ﷺ) guide each student personally.
masjid.founding_accent: The Hifz Program is registered under UP Waqf Board and UP Madrasa Education Board. 100% of donations fund student boarding and food programs.
masjid.docs_desc: All our registrations and legal documents are public. We believe full transparency builds trust.
masjid.verify_docs_desc: All original documents are available for inspection at our office. You can also contact the respective authorities to verify registration numbers. Email: info@madrasafarooqia.org
masjid.milestones_desc: 25 years of growth, transparency, and service to the community
masjid.mile1: Masjid & Hifz Program officially launched with 15 boarding students
masjid.mile2: First batch of 5 Huffaz graduated with complete memorisation
masjid.mile3: Dedicated residential hall constructed for Hifz boarding students
masjid.mile4: UP Waqf Board registration officially granted under Waqf No. 0892
masjid.mile5: UP Madrasa Education Board affiliation officially granted
masjid.mile6: Student count crossed 100 — launched dedicated Tajweed accent program
masjid.mile7: Quran Calligraphy course introduced for boarding students
masjid.mile8: First digital database launched for alumni Huffaz tracking
masjid.mile9: Masjid expansion completed — accommodating 150+ students in daily circles
masjid.mile10: Ijazah-chain teacher recruitment drive — hiring scholars from premium institutions
masjid.mile11: Silver Jubilee (25 years) — 500+ total graduated Huffaz served
masjid.courses_desc: Structured pathways to achieve complete memorisation and strong retention
masjid.course1_desc: Residential Quran memorisation program with daily testing
masjid.course2_desc: Pronunciation, articulation, and theoretical rules of Quran recitation
masjid.course3_desc: Structured daily revision for graduated Huffaz to retain memorisation
masjid.faculty_desc: Scholars holding authentic Ijazah chains for Quran memorisation and Tajweed
masjid.faq1_q: What is the average duration to complete Hifz?
masjid.faq1_a: It usually takes between 3 to 4 years for a student to complete memorisation, depending on their individual capacity, discipline, and daily retention.
masjid.faq2_a: Yes. We offer fully-managed boarding houses and nutritious meals for all residential Hifz students on campus, funded entirely by donor support.
masjid.faq3_q: How is the quality of revision maintained?
masjid.faq3_a: Each student has a daily Daur (revision) cycle. Before graduating, every student must recite the entire Quran in large portions to a committee of senior teachers to receive their Ijazah certificate.
masjid.faq4_a: Yes. You can sponsor a student's education, food, and boarding for a month, a year, or their entire journey. It is a beautiful form of Sadqa Jariyah.
masjid.support_girls_education_desc: Sponsor a Hifz boarding student — an ongoing Sadqa Jariyah for you and your family.
masjid.sched6_act: Asr Prayer — Group Hifz Review with Teacher
masjid.sched8_act: Isha Prayer — Final Night Revision Session
